target_change: Make strict source-derived DeepWiki-scale breadth the core behaviour of the expanded wiki-generation path (not a separate opt-in product), harden the Phase 2 planner so it authors a fanned-out hierarchy, and carry promoted catalog-topic granularity into Phase 3/4.
deliverables: expanded mode enforces the anti-compression breadth gate by default; deepwiki-scale is a compatibility alias
source-derived breadth budget (page/required-topic targets, per-family fan-out floor) derived from the catalog and shown to the planner
hardened planner prompt/schema (embedded + Gem files) instructing fan-out, one TER per promoted catalog topic, and no broad page claiming leaf coverage
promoted-topic downstream data contract (plans/promoted-topic-contract.json) + catalog_topic_id carried into Phase 4 generated coverage
constraints: No live/billed Vertex/Gemini/API calls; no full real RAGFlow E2E in this run
Do not delete prior PRDs/TDDs; do not patch past generated artifacts
Python hard rules: explicit typing, no mutable defaults, no import-time side effects, DI for policy, deterministic tests, honest validation
uncertainty: Whether full Phase 4 enforcement that cross-references the promoted-topic contract end-to-end fits in one safe slice (decided: defer the standalone gate; ship the data contract + granularity passthrough + tests)
Exact BreadthPolicy thresholds for live RAGFlow-scale runs (defaults seeded; release owner sign-off required before billed runs)
known_unknowns: Live GPT-5.4/Gemini planner response quality to the hardened prompt (cannot be measured without a billed run)
assumptions: The Phase A topic catalog (derived/topic-catalog.json) is the source of breadth truth; benchmark is never read
Phase 3 already tags evidenced topic rows with catalog_topic_id in the expanded family (verified in code)
